title: Gerascanthus scabra
taxonomy: Gerascanthus scabra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Gerascanthus is part of the tribe Plumerieae, which includes plants native to tropical regions. This classification places Gerascanthus scabra within the broader group of Apocynaceae, a large and diverse family of plants. The family Apocynaceae contains over 5,000 species, many of which are woody vines or shrubs. Further classification of Gerascanthus scabra also includes its membership within the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the coffee and gentian families.

intro: Gerascanthus scabra, also known as the rough-stemmed Gerascanthus, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gerascanthus scabra include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also dried and used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
